topics.name: 100 Level: Legal Methods, Constitutional Law Foundations & Nigerian Legal System
topics.description: LAW 101 Legal Methods (UNN), LAW 111 Nigerian Legal System, GST 101 Communication in English.

subtopics.name: LAW 101: UNN Legal Methods and Case Analysis
objectives: Identify and apply rules of statutory interpretation as used by Nigerian courts
Brief decided cases and write structured legal arguments in the UNN Faculty of Law format

subtopics.name: LAW 111: Sources of Nigerian Law and Court Structure
objectives: Trace the historical development of Nigerian law through customary, Islamic, and received English law
Map the hierarchy of Nigerian courts and describe the jurisdiction and composition of each court tier

topics.name: 200–300 Level: Contract, Torts, Criminal Law, Constitutional Law & Land Law
topics.description: LAW 201 Law of Contract, LAW 211 Law of Torts, LAW 301 Criminal Law, LAW 311 Land Law Nigeria.

subtopics.name: LAW 201 & LAW 211: Contract and Torts at UNN
objectives: Identify elements of contract formation, performance, breach, and available remedies under Nigerian law
Explain duty of care, negligence, strict liability, and occupiers' liability under Nigerian tort law

subtopics.name: LAW 301 & LAW 311: Criminal Law and Land Law
objectives: Analyse criminal offences under the Criminal Code (South), Penal Code (North), and EFCC/ICPC Acts
Apply the Land Use Act 1978 to resolve disputes over statutory rights of occupancy and customary tenure

topics.name: 400–500 Level: Commercial Law, Evidence, ADR, Law Clinic & Research Project
topics.description: LAW 401 Company Law, LAW 411 Evidence, LAW 421 ADR, LAW 501 Law Clinic, LAW 599 Research.

subtopics.name: LAW 401 & LAW 411: Company Law and Evidence
objectives: Explain corporate formation, governance, and winding-up under CAMA 2020
Apply admissibility rules, hearsay exceptions, and electronic evidence under the Evidence Act 2011

subtopics.name: LAW 501 & LAW 599: Clinical Programme and LLB Research Dissertation
objectives: Represent clients under supervision in UNN Law Clinic, draft agreements, letters of demand, and affidavits
Submit and orally defend a 10,000-word research dissertation before the UNN Faculty of Law board
